summary: Typosquat of Codebuff CLI fetches and executes opaque binary from unrelated GitHub account
details: Package name and README impersonate the CodebuffAI product (README is copied from CodebuffAI/codebuff and instructs users to `npm install -g codebuff`), but the bin script in cli/bin/codebuff.cjs downloads release binaries from `github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli` — a personal account unrelated to the impersonated publisher. On first invocation of the `codebuff` / `codebuff-cli` command, the script fetches `codebuff-<platform>-<arch>` from that third-party GitHub releases URL, writes it to `~/.codebuff/bin/`, `chmod 0755`s it, and spawns it with the user's argv. No hash or signature verification is performed on the downloaded bytes. There are no npm lifecycle scripts, so the dropper fires at first CLI invocation rather than at `npm install`, but the README directs users to run the CLI immediately. The combination — name/README impersonation of an established product plus an unverified binary download from an unrelated maintainer's GitHub account that is then executed — lets whoever controls that GitHub account ship arbitrary native code to anyone who installs the lookalike package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
